vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. P1 (Yellow Pot) and P2/P4 (Black Pot) show no visible foliage. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor but lacks the expected Pothos foliage.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; no Pothos foliage detected.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dark and potentially over-saturated or compacted.
biome_observations.debris: White crystalline fragments (likely perlite or mineral buildup) visible on the surface of P3.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: A single, thin, pale green sprout is visible in P3, likely an opportunistic weed or remnant germination, not the registered Pothos.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t1: Total loss of visible plant structure; transition to complete dormancy or death.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Stagnation; the single sprout in P3 remains the only biological activity.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable for the registered species. The absence of foliage across all pots indicates a catastrophic failure of the indoor environment or a complete senescence of the specimens.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of a singular, uncatalogued sprout in P3; total absence of registered flora.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a system in collapse. All registered specimens (P1-P4) are absent, suggesting either total plant mortality or removal. The environment is currently characterized by bare, dark soil. The only biological indicator is a solitary, unidentified sprout in the P3 pot, which does not match the morphology of the registered Pothos. The white rabbit anchor remains as the sole consistent element in the P3 pot.
